drafts.gaps: None known. All lemmas have complete elementary proofs with explicit two-sided bijections; edge cases (n=1, a=1, a=n, i=a-1, empty tail, S_II empty for n<=2) handled in-text. Two independent Codex hostile passes found no mathematical gap and no counterexample; its three minor wording findings (a=n wording in Obs 5.0(i), spelled-out (A_i) case, existence of next step in Lemma C) are incorporated. Remaining caveats are only the generic ones: correctness rests on the pinned conventions in DEFINITIONS.md matching the paper (they are quoted verbatim from the published LaTeX), and the proof has not yet been formalized in Lean (Section 8 Remark 4 records design choices made for that purpose).

None known. Every lemma has a written proof and a matching exhaustive numeric test: word-level set equality avoiders == construction for n=2..10 (57514 words at n=10; counts at n=9,10 are two levels beyond the paper's n<=8 check); per-h window products vs brute force for all eps, n<=9; m-vector counts vs shape counts n<=9; closed form vs independent DP for all 2^14 eps at n<=15; sum identity to n<=22; fuzz at n=12..16 (200k random nonnesting words) and n=20/30/40 (9k constructions). Residual risk is confined to prose-level slips in the Theorem 10 product argument, which is exactly the part double-covered by Test A and by Codex's independent checker.

None known. Every lemma machine-verified exhaustively beyond its proof: L2/L4 and the full characterization checked on every (shape,perm) pair for n<=7 (2,162,160 pairs at n=7, exact avoider-SET equality); component factorization and classification checked against brute constraint-system solving for every Dyck shape n<=8 and every irreducible component with <=8 arcs; per-shape predicted counts match the independent Rust ground truth (refined_stats.json) for all 1430 shapes at n=8 including all zero shapes; GF identities verified symbolically (sympy) and coefficientwise (components to m=11, series to z^24); transfer matrix equals the closed form for n<=40. Honest caveats (not gaps): the 5-case analysis in Lemma 2 and the index bookkeeping in Lemmas 4-7 are the only places an error could hide, which is precisely what V1-V5 test exhaustively; Lemma 4 is only claimed for prefix-extremal p (all that is needed).

None known. Every map is explicit in both directions with pointwise inverse proofs; case analyses are exhaustive and mutually exclusive; edge cases (n=1, a=1, a=n-1, i=1, i=a-1, empty tails, all-C tails) handled in-proof. Numeric validation: Phi well-defined/injective with image exactly W_n on all 85,514 brute-force avoiders for n=1..10 (n<=5 re-derived from the literal containment definition, n<=7 from all n!Cat_n pairs, n=9,10 from an independent pruned DFS — beyond the paper's n<=8); Psi total on W_n with every output verified a genuine avoider; both W_n descriptions (predicate vs complement) agree on the full 3^n cube for n<=10. Caveats only: the n=9,10 ground truth uses the fast avoidance checks (validated against the literal definition at n<=5), and the shared foundation (Lemmas 0-2, Theorem A) leans on the same arguments as dyck-sign.md (independently Codex-vetted twice, plus clean-room re-verification n<=6 in this thread).

None known. Every lemma is proved with explicit maps/case analyses, and every structural claim was verified numerically far beyond the original n<=8 ground truth: Theorem A word-level exhaustive n<=7 (2,162,160 pairs at n=7, by me) and exhaustive n=8 (all 57,657,600 pairs, 0 mismatches, by Codex's independent clean-room Rust harness); shape classification, forest structure, and per-shape count formula exhaustive over all Dyck shapes n<=13 (742,900 shapes at n=13); randomized counterexample hunts at n=9,10,11 (2M uniform (shape, arbitrary perm) + 2M uniform (shape, Av(132,312)-perm) samples per n, 0 mismatches); final summation identity checked exactly for n<=60 and by hand. Residual risk is limited to prose-level slips, not substance; the draft's Section 9 lists the five subtlest steps and their resolutions for a human referee.

None known. The audit-risk spots are flagged in the draft's section 12: the many-case analysis of Lemma 3 (each case spelled out and exhaustively machine-checked to n=7, randomly at n=8,9,10), exhaustiveness of the Lemma 10 classification (verified for every irreducible component up to 12 arcs by two independent solvers, 13 by Codex), and the dummy-sign factor 2 (eps_1 provably never constrained). Appendix Proposition A (the kernel-walk bijection) is proof-sketch only, but it is explicitly outside the formal chain and used purely as numeric cross-validation.

judge: VERDICT: two drafts are complete and audit-ready; no draft is gapped. All six prove the theorem through the same load-bearing core (FIFO normal form; sign-word encoding of prefix-extremal labels; the characterization 'avoider iff prefix-interval labels + every arc opening after the first closer has sign opposite to all arcs open at its opener'), differing only in endgame. PICKS: (1) recurrence-structural.md — primary. Most compact full proof, fully elementary (no GFs, no graph theory), deepest verification of any draft: Theorem A checked exhaustively word-level for all pairs n<=7 AND fully exhaustively at n=8 (all 57,657,600 pairs, Codex clean-room Rust, 0 mismatches — the only draft with a complete n=8 word-level exhaustive), shape classification to n=13, randomized hunts n=9-11, summation exact to n=60. (2) bijection.md — secondary, chosen for endgame independence and Lean-friendliness: its Sections 1-4 are the same (already triple-reviewed) foundation, but Sections 5-9 replace all counting/summation with an explicit two-way bijection onto a ternary language whose cardinality is a two-line disjointness count; verified end-to-end on all 85,514 avoiders n<=10 (beyond the paper's n<=8) and on the full 3^n cube. Auditing the shared core once plus the two disjoint endgames gives redundancy: if the core and EITHER endgame survive, the theorem stands; lemma items 1-4 of pick 2 are explicit cross-references to pick 1's items to avoid duplicate audit work. INDEPENDENT SPOT-CHECK (this triage, clean-room code written from the draft statements only, work/triage_spotcheck.py): literal biconditional containment vs the drafts' fast criteria and FIFO-vs-literal nonnesting agree (n<=4 all words; n=5 all 5040 nonnesting); Theorem A predicate == brute avoidance on ALL (s,p) pairs n<=7 (2,162,160 at n=7, 0 mismatches), counts match the formula; per-shape Lemma B formula == eps-brute per shape n<=8 and totals == formula n<=10; bijection Phi injective with image exactly W_n n<=7. All passed. NOT PICKED: dyck-sign.md is the same proof as pick 1 with more prose (its content is fully covered by the two picks — it is the textual source of pick 2's foundation); transfer-matrix.md and kernel-cluster.md are sound-looking but route through formal-power-series/cluster assembly, disfavored for the Lean target, and their component-classification index bookkeeping (Lemmas 6-7 / 8-10) is the longest at-risk stretch; generating-tree.md is correct-looking but its Theorem 10 per-h window-product argument is the subtlest counting step in the folder (highest audit risk per page) and is now redundant. CAVEATS FOR THE ORCHESTRATOR: (a) three input reports share the id 'Elizalde-Luo' — the path field is authoritative for pick 2 (drafts/bijection.md); (b) conventions are pinned locally in DEFINITIONS.md quoting the published DMTCS LaTeX verbatim (src/formatted.tex, DOI 10.46298/dmtcs.14885) — I verified the pinned containment/nonnesting text directly rather than refetching the paper; (c) recommended audit order: pick-1 items 1-7 (shared core, highest priority: Theorem A's case analysis), then pick-1 items 8-13 (summation endgame), then pick-2 items 5-13 (bijection endgame). If the audit must choose a single Lean target, pick 2's endgame is the cheaper formalization (explicit maps + pointwise inverses + one language count; no summation algebra), sitting on pick 1's core.

audits.lemmaVerdicts: 1. Lemma 0 (FIFO): SOUND. Both directions valid; (<=) uses sigma=id correctly, (=>) any non-identity sigma has an inversion i<j and o_i<o_j<q_sigma(j)<q_sigma(i) is a genuine nesting (o_j<q_sigma(j) holds for every matched pair = fst<snd); o_i<q_i proved from prefix counts. One PRESENTATIONAL micro-gap: the proof establishes 'nonnesting iff sigma=id' but the statement is letter-equality; the bridge (w[o_i]=w[q_i] for all i <=> sigma=id) is implicit — it needs exactly-two-copies + o_i being a first occurrence (one line). Verified vs literal 1221/2112 containment on all words n<=5 (116,017 words, 0 mismatches). 2. Arc-support fact: SOUND. Position x>q_i lies in some arc J whose last position q_J>=x>q_i, and q sorted gives J>i; trivially arc i's letters are at o_i,q_i<=q_i. Used correctly ('J,K>i') in Theorem A. 3. Lemma 1: SOUND. (=>) biconditional convention forces w_{i1}=w_{i2}=a, two-copies forces {i1,i2}={fst(a),snd(a)}, i2=snd(a); strict relations transfer. (<=) the quadruple (fst(a),snd(a),k,l) realizes ALL pairwise order/equality relations of 1132 (incl. the needed non-equalities w_k,w_l != a and w_k != w_l, all strict by hypothesis). Dual 3312 symmetric. Verified vs literal containment, all words n<=5, 0 mismatches. 4. Lemma 2: SOUND. Pure disjunction-merge of Lemma 1(1)+(2); 'strictly between' covers exactly the two cases. Verified same scope. 5. Lemma 3: SOUND. Closers increase with arc index by definition of q-sorting + Lemma 0 (snd(p_i)=q_i); triple (p_i,q_j,q_k) satisfies snd(v)<x<y and betweenness; both stated directions are contrapositives of the one implication proved. Verified n<=7 (every avoider has prefix-interval labels). 6. Lemma 4: SOUND. (1)=>(2)=>(3)=>(1) each airtight (for k>=2 prefix min<max so eps well-defined and exclusive); reconstruction p_1=1+#L correct (final prefix interval [p_1-#L,p_1+#H]=[1,n]); mutual inverseness by induction; (*) exhaustive/exclusive since eps_j in {L,H} and values distinct. Verified all p in S_n, n<=8, incl. Phi bijectivity and (*) for all i<j. (Caution: my first run flagged this lemma — bug in MY checker, not the draft.) 7. THEOREM A: SOUND (highest scrutiny). Triple decomposition exhaustive: v=p_i with snd(v)=q_i (Lemma 0); x,y>q_i forces arcs J,K>i (arc-support), J!=K from strict betweenness; case K>J killed by prefix-extremality of p_K over a set containing p_i,p_J; case K<J: x=q_J impossible (y>x but arc K ends q_K<q_J), y=o_K impossible (o_K<o_J=x), so x=o_J,y=q_K forced, with o_J<q_K and q_i<o_J; betweenness<=>eps_K=eps_J via (*) in both H/L branches, and the equivalence is UNIFORM in i (p_i<p_K <=> eps_K=H for every i<K), so the exists-i elimination (exists i<K with q_i<o_J <=> q_1<o_J; K>=2 automatic from q_1<o_J<q_K, so eps_K defined) is legitimate and loses no direction. Bijection statement correctly assembled from Lemmas 0/3/4; n=1 vacuous case handled. Machine-verified: literal-definition triple cross-check ALL (s,p) n<=6 (95,040 at n=6) and full n=7 (2,162,160 pairs), 0 mismatches; Codex hostile referee: AIRTIGHT. 8. Prop F1: SOUND. Pair selection always yields K_1<K_2 in S_J with K_2>a (larger of any witness pair >a works); K_1 in S_{K_2} since q_{K_1}>o_J>o_{K_2}; all three pairs genuine (C)-pairs (K_2>a and J>a give q_1<o; memberships checked); 3 letters pairwise distinct in {L,H} impossible; K_1>=2 since 1 notin S_J. Verified n<=12. 9. Props F2-F3: SOUND. F2: o_J>q_1 <=> J>a (from 5.1); a in S_{J_2} (a<J_2, o_{J_2}<q_a); J_1 in S_{J_2} (J_1>=a+1 so q_{J_1}>=q_{a+1}>q_a>o_{J_2}); reduces to F1. F3: K in S_J => q_K>o_J>q_a => K>a, so |S_J|>=2 violates F1. Verified n<=12. 10. Canonical form 5.2: SOUND. Single gap opener must be o_{a+1} (openers ordered: if any opener in (q_1,q_a) then o_{a+1} is, F2 forces uniqueness); 1<=i<=a-1 with a>=2 forced by strict betweenness; all other positions in (q_1,q_a) are closers; tail-U-height<=1 from F3 + |S_J|=height; converse: glued path is Dyck, first run exactly a (D follows since i>=1 resp. a<n... a=n has empty tail), parameters recovered uniquely (a, presence/position of gap U, suffix after q_a all intrinsic). Edge cases a=n, a=1, tau=D all fine. Verified: N(s)>0 <=> canonical, and gap opener always arc a+1, all shapes n<=12. 11. Section 5.3: SOUND, one wording caveat. Inventory exact: S_B={i+1..a} (m_B-1=i closers before o_B); height-1 tail openers have S_J={J-1} with J-1>a (q_{J-1}>o_J>q_a); height-0 tail S_J empty; first-run arcs appear only in S_B, arc 1 in none (tail S_J subset of late arcs). The count is valid AS WRITTEN because the draft explicitly collapses the bridge star to one block bit c before multiplying ('block constant = c, eps_B = not-c'); after that, every forced variable (block members, eps_B, height-1 tail) depends only on strictly earlier data, including chains attaching to B itself (B<J). CAVEAT: Section 9 item 4's gloss 'no variable is bound twice' is literally false pre-collapse (eps_B is incident to a-i constraints) — commentary wording only, proof body unaffected; suggest the one-line repair. Inventory + formula verified exactly for every admissible shape n<=12 (e.g. 208,012 shapes at n=12). 12. Section 5.4: SOUND. Heights never exceed 2 (induction: U only from <=1); at height 0 first step forced U with weight 2 (z counts absolute height-0 U-steps, preserved by decomposition since remainder keeps absolute heights); at height 2 first step forced D (m>=1 path nonempty); bases f(0,h)=1 incl. f(0,2)=DD; closed forms by induction (f(m,1)=3f(m-1,1) using f(m-1,2)=f(m-1,1) valid at m-1=0 too). Verified: direct path enumeration = recurrence = closed form, m<=8 all h, and DP to m=39 range covered by summation check. 13. Section 5.5: SOUND. Index ranges correct (bridge a in [2,n-1], arc budget a+1+m=n; no-bridge a in [1,n]); a=n term 2^{n-1} isolated correctly; geometric identity proved by induction and re-verified; Sigma_2 closed form 3^{n-1}-2^n+1 also vanishes at n=1,2 matching empty sum; final algebra exact. Verified: identity exact n<=40; class-sum = brute per-shape sum n<=9; totals = formula n<=12 and = ground-truth avoider counts n<=8 (data/counts.json).
audits.notes: Adversarial audit (logician lens) found NO invalid deductive step and NO false lemma. Every iff was checked in both directions; every case split checked for exhaustiveness; every membership/inequality computation in F1-F3 and Theorem A re-derived by hand. Two PRESENTATIONAL micro-gaps, both with one-line fixes, neither affecting validity: (1) Lemma 0's proof establishes 'nonnesting <=> sigma=id' while the statement is letter-equality at matching opener/closer indices; the bridging equivalence (uses exactly-two-copies + o_i being a first occurrence) is implicit and should be stated. (2) Section 9 item 4's summary phrase 'no variable is bound twice' is literally false before the bridge-star collapse (eps_B is bound by a-i constraints); Section 5.3's actual argument already does the collapse correctly ('block = c, eps_B = not-c'), so this is a commentary wording fix, not a proof repair. Independent clean-room verification (scripts at C:\Users\jacks\source\repos\maths\problems\p3-moonshot\elizalde-luo\work\audit_logician\audit1.py and audit2.py, log audit2.log): Lemmas 0-2 vs LITERAL biconditional containment on all 116,017 words n<=5; Lemma 4 + Phi + (*) all p in S_n n<=8; Theorem A word-level vs literal definition full n=6 (95,040 pairs) and vs validated criteria full n=7 (2,162,160 pairs) — 0 mismatches, avoider counts 634/1996 = formula; shape level n<=12: N(s) formula, admissible<=>canonical, F1-F3, and the exact 5.3 constraint-pair inventory hold for all 208,012 shapes at n=12; tail DP and summation identity exact to n=40. Disclosure: my first audit run flagged Lemma 4 — traced to a position-role bug in MY OWN 312-checker; after fixing, zero failures (draft vindicated). Codex hostile-referee round (thread 019eba39-bbe2-7b70-88ed-4542e203a15b): 'no kill'; Theorem A and 5.3 both judged AIRTIGHT; it independently confirmed the two micro-gaps above and proposed the same minimal repairs. Recommended edits before Lean formalization: add the Lemma 0 bridging sentence; reword Section 9 item 4 to 'after replacing the bridge star by the single block parameter c, the remaining system is triangular'; optionally add Codex's suggested explicit bijection sentence after the 5.3 product count. With those cosmetic edits the draft is ready for formalization.

audits.lemmaVerdicts:
1. Lemma 0 (FIFO normal form): SOUND. Both directions re-derived by hand (inversion of sigma yields explicit nest via o_i < o_j < q_sigma(j) < q_sigma(i); o_j < q_sigma(j) is fst<snd of one value). Bijection round-trip verified for all n!*Cat_n pairs n<=6; literal 1221/2112 scan == FIFO criterion for ALL 113,400 multiset words at n=5 (and all n<=4).
2. Arc-support fact: SOUND. For an opener o_J > q_i: o_i < q_i < o_J forces J > i since openers sorted; for a closer q_J > q_i: J > i since closers sorted (q_J > q_i <=> J > i is trivial monotonicity of the sorted closer list). Verified for every position of every shape n<=8, 0 violations.
3. Lemma 1 (positional criterion): SOUND. (=>) exactly-two-copies forces {i1,i2} = {fst(a),snd(a)}, i2 = snd(a); (<=) all six pairwise biconditional relations of (1,1,3,2)/(3,3,1,2) checked strict. Literal quadruple-scan containment == criterion for all words n<=4, all 5040 nonnesting + 30,000 random (incl. nesting) words n=5: 0 mismatches.
4. Lemma 2 (combined criterion): SOUND. Direct disjunction of Lemma 1(1)/(2); O(L^3) triple scan == (literal 1132 or literal 3312) on the same corpus, 0 mismatches.
5. Lemma 3 (label necessity): SOUND. Construction (v,x,y)=(p_i,q_j,q_k) satisfies Lemma 2's hypotheses since closers increase (q_i<q_j<q_k) and arc j's closer carries letter p_j (Lemma 0). Verified word-level n<=6: no avoider has p containing 132/312.
6. Lemma 4 (sign coordinates): SOUND. Three-way equivalence, Phi/Phi^{-1} mutual inverseness (both compositions), surjectivity onto {L,H}^{n-1}, |Av_n|=2^{n-1}, and rule (*) (exhaustive/exclusive: eps_j=H => p_j prefix-max > p_i; eps_j=L => prefix-min < p_i) all verified for every p in S_n, n<=7, and every eps, 0 failures.
7. THEOREM A: SOUND. Highest-scrutiny item. Hand re-derivation: (i) x,y > q_i lie on arcs J,K > i (arc-support); J != K by strictness; (ii) K>J killed by prefix-extremality of p_K in {p_1..p_K} (i,J < K); (iii) K<J forces x=o_J (x=q_J impossible: both K-positions < q_J), y=q_K (o_K < o_J), giving q_i < o_J < q_K; (iv) betweenness <=> eps_K=eps_J via (*) is i-INDEPENDENT because (*) applied to (i,K) gives p_i<p_K <=> eps_K=H for any i<K — this is the linchpin and it holds; (v) exists-i elimination: i=1 works iff q_1<o_J; K>=2 automatic. Machine: predicted==actual for ALL 2,162,160 (s,p) at n=7 (clean-room, 0 mismatches, independent of the draft's and Codex's harnesses), 400k random samples n=9,10 (0 mismatches), PLUS a new internal-structure check: every one of the 16,000+ violating triples at n<=6 realizes exactly the claimed (i<K<J, x=o_J, y=q_K, q_i<o_J<q_K, eps_K=eps_J) form — the proof's case analysis itself, not just its conclusion, is exhaustively confirmed. Codex hostile round: AIRTIGHT on all four sub-items.
8. Proposition F1: SOUND. Pair selection always possible (larger of any two elements with one > a is > a); K_1 in S_{K_2} via o_{K_2} < o_J < q_{K_1}; all three pairs are genuine (C)-pairs (K_2, J both late => q_1 < opener); 3 pairwise-distinct constraints on 2 colors is a contradiction. Verified as stated for every shape with N(s)>0, n<=12: 0 violations.
9. Propositions F2-F3: SOUND. F2: a in S_{J_2} (o_{J_2} < q_a, a < J_2) and J_1 in S_{J_2} (q_{J_1} >= q_{a+1} > q_a > o_{J_2}); F1 applies since J_1 > a. F3: K in S_J => q_K > o_J > q_a => K > a; reduction to F1 correct. Verified n<=12, 0 violations.
10. Canonical form (5.2): SOUND. Single gap opener must be o_{a+1} (smallest opener > q_1; it is <= the witness opener < q_a); strict betweenness gives 1 <= i <= a-1 (needs a >= 2, stated); no late closer before q_a (closers sorted); parameters (a, bridge?, i, tau) uniquely recovered — my parser rebuilds s from parameters and string-compares, exact round-trip for every admissible shape n<=12. Class equality {N(s)>0} == {canonical} exact for all 290,512 shapes n<=12. Edge cases a=n, a=1, bridge with tau=D all hit and pass.
11. Section 5.3 (per-shape count): SOUND. Inventory exactness verified per-shape n<=12 including the sharpened form: bridge S_B = {i+1..a} exactly; every height-1 tail opener J has S_J = {J-1} with J-1 > a (S_J is an up-interval ending at J-1 since closers increase — so a singleton S_J is automatically {J-1}); height-0 tail openers and arcs 2..a (resp. 2..i) constraint-free; arc 1 in no S_J. Triangularity: the only candidate double-binding (eps_B bound by block AND by tail constraint from J=a+2) does not occur because the tail constraint forces eps_J, not its earlier source eps_B — confirmed by hand and by Codex. N(s) formula == brute-force-over-2^{n-1}-eps count (n<=8) == bipartite-component count (n<=12), 0 mismatches across all shapes.
12. Section 5.4 (tail counts): SOUND. Heights bounded by 2 (induction); forced U at height 0 (weight 2), forced D at height 2; bases f(0,h)=1 incl. the subtle f(0,1)='D', f(0,2)='DD'. Direct path enumeration == recurrence == closed forms for m<=11, recurrence == closed form m<=300. Weight transport is absolute-height bookkeeping, no issue.
13. Section 5.5 (final summation): SOUND. Index ranges correct (bridge: 2<=a<=n-1, m=n-a-1; no-bridge: 1<=a<=n, m=n-a); geometric identity and boundary terms re-derived by hand; summation == 3^n - 3*2^{n-1} + 1 exactly for n<=200, AND a direct enumeration over the (a, optional i, tau) parameter space (explicitly generating tails) == formula for n<=12, AND the per-shape sum of bipartite N(s) == formula for n<=12. End-to-end fully-literal count (quadruple-scan definitions only) == formula for n<=5; criterion-based for n=6,7.
audits.notes: Adversarial combinatorialist audit: I could not break any bijection or decomposition. Method: (1) hand re-derivation of every quantifier/case step, specifically hunting for the classic failure modes (triple decomposition missing a case with x=q_J; i-dependence of the betweenness computation when i is not minimal; eps_B double-binding making the product count wrong; gap opener not o_{a+1}; double counting in the (a,i,tau) parametrization) — none materialize, each is excluded by sorted-openers/sorted-closers monotonicity; (2) clean-room verification suite written from DEFINITIONS.md only (not reusing draft/work code): C:\Users\jacks\source\repos\maths\problems\p3-moonshot\elizalde-luo\work\audit_comb2\audit.py, log heavy.log — 12 stages, ALL PASS, including two checks beyond prior rounds: stage D2 verifies the INTERNAL structure of Theorem A's proof (every violating triple matches the claimed decomposition exactly, n<=6), and stage F verifies the full constraint inventory + F1-F3 + classification + N(s) formula per-shape to n=12 with totals matching the closed formula; (3) hostile Codex (GPT-5.5) referee round, thread 019eba3b-e096-7420-be25-19edf1dcdde0, targeted at the two weakest steps (Theorem A triple decomposition/exists-i elimination; 5.2-5.3 canonical form + inventory triangularity incl. the eps_B double-binding scenario): verdict AIRTIGHT on every sub-item, no other gaps found. Non-blocking drafting suggestions for the Lean pass: (a) state explicitly in 5.2 that o_{a+1} <= (any opener in (q_1,q_a)) < q_a, hence the gap opener is o_{a+1}; (b) in 5.3 add one sentence that a height-1 tail constraint forces eps_J and never re-binds its source (which may itself be the forced eps_B); (c) the draft's tail-constraint statement S_J = {K}, K > a can be sharpened to S_J = {J-1} (closers increase => S_J is an up-interval ending at J-1), which is what the formalization should use. No counterexample exists in any verified range (words: all n<=7 + n=8 by prior exhaustive Rust; shapes: all n<=13 prior, n<=12 re-verified here independently).

audits.lemmaVerdicts:
1. Lemma 0 (FIFO normal form): SOUND. Both directions check out analytically (sigma != id has an inversion i<j; the arc opened at o_j nests inside the one opened at o_i via o_i < o_j < q_sigma(j) < q_sigma(i), using o_j < q_sigma(j) which holds for every matched fst<snd pair; converse direct). Exactly-two-copies is used and available; o_i < q_i is the standard prefix-dominance fact. Fresh enumeration: literal 1221/2112 biconditional containment == FIFO criterion on ALL multiset permutations n<=5 (116,017 words incl. nesting ones), 0 failures; nonnesting totals = n!*Cat_n for n<=5.
2. Arc-support fact: SOUND. Trivial since closers are sorted by arc index (q_J > q_i <=> J > i is immediate from the sorted list); verified on every nonnesting word n<=5 (arc i never has a letter past q_i; every position > q_i belongs to an arc J > i), 0 failures.
3. Lemma 1 (positional criterion): SOUND. The biconditional convention forces i2 = snd(a) (exactly-two-copies); the <= direction's quadruple satisfies all strict order/equality relations of 1132 (resp. 3312). Verified literal-containment vs criterion on ALL words (not just nonnesting) n<=5, both patterns, 0 mismatches.
4. Lemma 2 (combined criterion): SOUND. Pure disjunction of Lemma 1's two cases; 'strictly between' is exactly the union. Verified on all words n<=5, 0 mismatches.
5. Lemma 3 (label necessity): SOUND. Closers increase in arc index (Lemma 0/fact 2); the triple (p_i, q_j, q_k) satisfies snd(p_i)=q_i < q_j < q_k and betweenness, meeting Lemma 2's hypotheses. Verified: every actual avoider at n<=7 has prefix-interval labels, 0 violations.
6. Lemma 4 (sign coordinates): SOUND. (1)<=>(2)<=>(3) airtight; reconstruction p_1 = 1+#L is forced by final interval = [1,n]; mutual inverseness by induction; (*) exhaustive/exclusive since for k>=2 the prefix has min<max and p_k is exactly one of them. Verified exhaustively for all n<=8 (all 40,320 perms at n=8): Av(132,312) = prefix-extreme = prefix-interval = image of reconstruction, Phi injective, rule (*) on all pairs, |Av| = 2^(n-1), 0 failures.
7. THEOREM A: SOUND. Triple decomposition exhaustive (v=p_i for some arc since every value is an arc label; x,y at arcs J,K>i by arc-support; K=J excluded by strictness; K>J killed by prefix-extremality since i,J<K; K<J forces x=o_J, y=q_K by the four-position combinatorics). The exists-i elimination is valid because betweenness <=> eps_K=eps_J holds UNIFORMLY in i: rule (*) gives p_i < p_K <=> eps_K=H for EVERY i<K, so the equivalence does not depend on the witness i, and i=1 works iff q_1 < o_J (K>=2 automatic from q_1 < o_J < q_K). Codex (hostile referee, thread 019eba38-6557-7061-9519-0dce9157e591) independently confirms: SOUND, with one wording nit (the 'such an i exists iff' sentence should explicitly carry the o_J < q_K side condition, which context supplies). Fresh exhaustive re-verification: predicted == actual for ALL (shape,perm) pairs n<=7 (2,162,160 pairs at n=7), 0 mismatches; avoider counts match formula 1,4,16,58,196,634,1996; also verified arc 1 never occurs in a (C)-pair.
8. Proposition F1: SOUND. Pair selection always possible (rename so the larger is the >a witness); K_1 in S_{K_2} via q_{K_1} > o_J > o_{K_2}; all three pairs genuine since K_2,J > a gives o > a+1 = q_1; 3 pairwise-distinct values in {L,H} impossible. Verified: F1 condition holds on every N(s)>0 shape, n<=11.
9. Propositions F2-F3: SOUND. Memberships a in S_{J_2} (o_a = a < q_1 < o_{J_2} < q_a) and J_1 in S_{J_2} (q_{J_1} >= q_{a+1} > q_a > o_{J_2}) both correct; reductions to F1 valid. Verified on all N(s)>0 shapes n<=11.
10. Canonical form (5.2): SOUND. The single gap opener must be o_{a+1} (openers sorted; o_{a+1} is the least opener > q_1, so any opener in (q_1,q_a) puts o_{a+1} there too); remaining positions in (q_1,q_a) are closers; parameters (a, optional i, tau) recovered uniquely. Edge cases a=n, a=1, bridge with tau=D all exercised. Verified: canonical <=> N(s)>0 for ALL Dyck shapes n<=11 (58,786 shapes at n=11), and the (a,i?,tau) parametrization generates each admissible shape exactly once (no collisions, set equality).
11. Section 5.3 (per-shape count): SOUND with a fixable PRESENTATION NIT. The constraint-inventory claim was verified as exact SET EQUALITY on every canonical shape n<=11: (C)-pairs = {(K,B): K=i+1..a} union {(J-1,J): J height-1 tail opener}, with S_J={J-1} and J-1>a always; no other constraints; first-run arcs 2..i (resp 2..a) and arc 1 in none. N(s) = 2^(a-1+z) / 2^(i+z) confirmed per-shape n<=11, cross-checked brute-force over all 2^(n-1) eps (n<=9) vs bipartite-component count (n<=11). NIT (Codex concurs): the phrase 'no variable is bound twice' is literally false for eps_B, which is hit by a-i inequations; the system is triangular only after collapsing the bridge star into 'block constant + eps_B = complement', which is what the draft's actual counting text does (correctly). One clarifying sentence would fix it; the count itself is right, and tail-vs-bridge consistency (J=a+2 with J-1=B) is automatic since eps_J is a fresh later variable.
12. Section 5.4 (tail counts): SOUND. Direct path enumeration == recurrence == closed forms for all m<=12, h in {0,1,2}; heights provably never exceed 2 (U only from height<=1); weight transport is by absolute height, consistent across the first-step decomposition; f(0,2)=1 (path DD) correct.
13. Section 5.5 (final summation): SOUND. Index ranges correct (bridge a<=n-1 forced by arc count; m=n-a-1); geometric identity and boundary terms verified; re-derived the algebra independently (Sigma_2 = 3^(n-1)-2^n+1 confirmed); identity checked as exact integers for n<=60; independently, sum over shapes of N(s) computed from scratch equals 3^n-3*2^(n-1)+1 for all n<=11 (174,076 at n=11, beyond the draft's word-level scope).
audits.notes: Adversarial computational audit found NO errors and NO gaps; I could not break the proof. Conventions were re-checked verbatim against the published LaTeX source (src/formatted.tex lines 117-138, 1599-1621) and match the draft's pinned DEFINITIONS.md. All numerics were re-verified with FRESH clean-room code (independent of the existing enumerators), in C:\Users\jacks\source\repos\maths\problems\p3-moonshot\elizalde-luo\work\audit-comp\: audit_word_level.py (literal biconditional containment over ALL multiset permutations n<=5: Lemmas 0,1,2 + arc-support + counts, 0 failures), audit_theoremA.py + audit_theoremA_fast.py (Theorem A exhaustive at (shape,perm) level n<=7 with literal cross-check n<=5 and fast-predicate self-test; 2,162,160 pairs at n=7, 0 mismatches), audit_inventory.py (Lemma 4 exhaustive n<=8; 5.3 constraint-inventory exact set-equality on all canonical shapes n<=11), audit_shapes.py (F1/F2/F3, canonical<=>N>0, parametrization bijection, N(s) formula, brute vs bipartite N, tail counts, summation; n<=11 shapes, n<=60 algebra). This independently extends the draft's own validation (shape-level sum now confirmed to n=11 = 174,076 by an implementation disjoint from the draft's work/ scripts). Codex (GPT-5.5, hostile-referee mode, thread 019eba38-6557-7061-9519-0dce9157e591) examined the two subtlest written steps at my request: Theorem A's exists-i elimination (verdict SOUND - the betweenness <=> eps_K=eps_J equivalence is uniform in i because rule (*) is universally quantified over i<K, so substituting i=1 is legitimate) and the 5.3 product count (verdict PRESENTATION-GAP only). Two cosmetic improvements recommended before Lean formalization, neither affecting correctness: (a) in Theorem A, add one sentence noting that the betweenness criterion is independent of the witness arc i (this is what licenses the quantifier elimination), and keep the o_J < q_K side condition explicit in the 'such an i exists iff' sentence; (b) in 5.3, replace 'no variable is bound twice' with an explicit two-stage statement: first collapse the bridge star {eps_K != eps_B : K=i+1..a} into the equivalent 'eps_{i+1}=...=eps_a = c and eps_B = not-c' (a one-free-bit subsystem), after which the remaining system is genuinely triangular with each height-1 tail variable bound exactly once by a strictly earlier variable. The draft's actual counting prose already performs exactly this collapse, so only the summarizing adjective is off. Ready for formalization as-is; the two wording fixes would make the audit trail cleaner.

audits.lemmaVerdicts:
1. Lemma 0 (FIFO bijection): SOUND. Biconditional-equality convention correctly forces {i1,i4}/{i2,i3} to be the two occurrence pairs; Step-2 double implication complete; Step-3 recovery argument gives uniqueness. Verified: T2 (full bijection + recovery, n<=4).
2. Lemma 1 + Corollary 1.1 (D_i/A_i criteria): SOUND. Both directions of each equivalence complete (all order/equality relations of 1132/3312 matched); Cor 1.1 correctly instantiates snd(p_i)=q_i via Lemma 0. Verified: T1 tests the Lemma-1 criterion against literal containment on ALL words of [n]_2 (nesting included) n<=4.
3. Facts 3.1-3.2 + Lemma 2: SOUND. p_1=1+#L is forced (min must reach 1); comparison rule exhaustive/exclusive; Lemma 2's minimal-j argument valid (h=p_j-+1 lies outside the prefix, so h=p_m with m>j; positions q_1<q_j<q_m violate D_1/A_1; hi>=p_1 holds since p_1 in I). Verified: T6 and standalone probe over all (s,p) pairs n<=5.
4. THEOREM A: SOUND. (=>) witness pair (o_J, q_K) both > q_1 valid; (<=) chain epsilon_B forced -> B<A -> epsilon_A forced -> t=o_A, t'=q_B, B in open(o_A), A late is airtight; A,B>=2 via A,B>i>=1. The open(o_J) formulation agrees with q_1<o_J<q_K by definition of open + lateness; index form J>=a+1 proved in Lemma 3. Verified: full equivalence n<=6 (T6) and all 2,162,160 pairs at n=7 (count 1996, set equality with independently generated avoider list).
5. Lemma 3 (FIFO interval): SOUND. Closers before o_J are an initial segment because q_1<q_2<... makes {m: q_m<o_J} downward closed; c<=J-1 justified; height formula correct. Verified: T5 on every Dyck word n<=8 including the lateness index form.
6. Lemma 4 (obstructions): SOUND. Openers in (q_1,q_a) form an index-contiguous block starting at a+1, so beta=a+1, beta'=a+2; 1<=c<=c'<=a-1 gives a in open(o_beta) and {a,beta} subset of open(o_beta'); a>=2 so epsilon_a defined. Tail case: K=J-2, K'=J-1 in open(o_J) with K>=2 by Observation 4.1; K' late via q_{K'}>o_J>q_a => K'>a; K in open(o_{K'}) via o_K<o_{K'}<q_K (q_K>o_J>o_{K'}). Verified: T7 checks every internal claim on every obstructed shape and absence of valid eps, n<=7.
7. Lemma 5 (S/I/II classification): SOUND. Forced reconstruction valid: tail arcs are the trailing block of openers, so between consecutive tail openers all positions are closers and D-run lengths are determined by start heights; converse direction's checks (Dyck property, U-count, first ascent exactly a since i>=1/a>=1 put a D at position a+1, gap-opener count, tail heights) all hold; edge cases a=n-1 (Tail(1;)=D), i=1, i=a-1, empty tails fine; trichotomy mutually exclusive (a=n vs a<n; gap opener absent vs present) and with Lemma 4 contrapositively exhaustive. Verified: T8 (classification, reconstruction s->params->s, and parameter generation exactly exhausting admissible shapes, n<=7). Codex hostile pass: SOUND.
8. Lemma 6 + Corollary 6.1: SOUND. open(o_{a+1})=empty in (I) (c=a at position 2a+1); delta_J=1 => open(o_J)={c+1..J-1} of size 1 = {J-1}; in (II) J=a+2 references a+1=B which is in F(s); extension map's processing order well-founded (j-1 in F, block, or earlier-processed tail); converse by induction on j. Verified: T9 (Lemma-6 predicate == Theorem-A condition on every admissible shape and every eps; restriction injective; valid count = 2^|F|; n<=7).
9. Lemma 7 (language count): SOUND. Violation iff X1 or X2; C-free string cannot start with C; 'no C followed later by non-C' correctly unwound to rho in {A,B}*C*; X1,X2 disjoint by first letter; (m,k) determined (v = maximal AB-prefix of rho); n=1 needs no special case. Verified: T10 predicate-vs-complement on every string of the cube, n<=8.
10. Theorem B step 1: SOUND. (II) witness pair r=i+1<=a (block nonempty since i<=a-1), t=a+1<=n, sigma_{a+1} a sign letter; three images pairwise disjoint by the C-free / sigma_1!=C-with-C / sigma_1=C trichotomy. Verified: T11 image in W_n and injectivity, n<=8.
11. Theorem B step 2 (Psi total): SOUND. Leading-C branch: r>=i+1 since positions 2..i are non-C; existence of a C at position >=2 gives i+1<=n and sigma_{i+1}=C; case split r<=a vs r>a exhaustive; r<=a forces t>=a+1 hence a<=n-1 and sigma_{a+1}!=C by run maximality; r>a gives a+1<=r<=n with r=a+1 impossible, same conclusion; recovered 1<=i<=a-1<=n-2 so Lemma 5(II) applies; outputs valid by Lemma 6 since forced equalities hold by construction. Verified: T11 (Psi total on all of W_n, asserts never fire, n<=8). Codex hostile pass: SOUND.
12. Theorem B steps 3-4: SOUND. (I) one-slot shift consistent between Phi (positions 1..a carry eps_2..eps_{a+1}) and Psi; induction recovering forced coordinates has correct base (eps_{a+1} directly recovered in both branches) and uses original validity only through Lemma 6's forced equalities — no circularity; (i,a) recovered exactly from the run structure in (II); step 4 writes back letter-by-letter. Minor presentational note (not a gap): step 4 tacitly uses that the rebuilt shape classifies into the same case — this is exactly what Lemma 5's converse states ('yields a Dyck word of case (I)/(II)'), but an explicit citation there would help the Lean port. Verified: T11 both inverse identities n<=8, T12 end-to-end n<=7, plus both Section-10 worked examples re-derived against the literal containment definition.
13. Main theorem: SOUND. Middle set equals Phi's domain: obstructed shapes carry no valid eps (Lemma 4) and non-obstructed shapes are exactly S/I/II (Lemma 5); the two hypotheses are logical complements, so coverage of all Dyck words is exhaustive. Verified: T12 (avoider set <-> W_n set equality both directions, n<=7) and T11 (domain count = |W_n|, n<=8).
audits.notes: Adversarial audit (logician lens) of drafts/bijection.md found NO invalid deductive step, no hidden assumption, and no missed degenerate case across all 13 items. Method: (1) line-by-line hand verification of every inference, with special attention to the flagged subtleties — Lemma 4's identification beta=a+1, beta'=a+2 and the tail-case lateness chain q_{K'}>o_J>q_a => K'>a (with K,K'>=2 via Observation 4.1); Lemma 3's initial-segment claim (immediate from q monotone); Lemma 5's forced-D-run reconstruction and converse; Theorem B step 2's two-case witness analysis; step 3's induction base. (2) Clean-room numeric harness work/audit_logician/audit.py (implements draft definitions directly, no project helper reuse): ALL TESTS PASS — T1 literal-definition equivalence of the Lemma-1 criteria on all words n<=4; T2 Lemma 0 n<=4; T5 Lemma 3 n<=8; T6 Theorem A full equivalence n<=6; T789 Lemmas 4/5/6 + Cor 6.1 internal claims on every Dyck shape n<=7; T10 Lemma 7 n<=8; T11 Theorem B two-sided inverse and image=W_n n<=8; T12 end-to-end n<=7. Extended: Theorem A over all Cat(7)*7! = 2,162,160 pairs at n=7 (predicted count 1996 = formula; predicted set identical to the independently generated avoider list; all 1996 re-verified by the validated fast checks). Extra probes: Section-10 n=3 table (16 rows) and the n=6 CBCCAC example re-derived from scratch and checked against the LITERAL containment definition; Lemma 2 standalone over all pairs n<=5. (3) Codex hostile second referee (fresh thread 019eba35-99d5-7b30-86d3-f74faa5954bb) attacked the two steps I judged weakest — Lemma 5 + exhaustiveness, and Theorem B steps 2-4 — verdict SOUND on both, plus its own counterexample search through n<=8 finding nothing. Only finding of any kind: a presentational nit in Theorem B step 4 (explicitly cite Lemma 5's converse for same-case classification of the rebuilt shape — already implied by Lemma 5's statement; Codex independently flagged the same sentence as a Lean-port nicety). Housekeeping: fixed a pure test-script bug in work/audit_logician/audit_n7.py (random.sample of 2000 from a 1996-word population raised ValueError; replaced by checking all 1996 words — the two mathematical assertions had already passed before the crash). Verdict: the proof is sound as written; ready for Lean formalization with the one optional clarifying sentence in Theorem B step 4.

audits.lemmaVerdicts: 1. Lemma 0 (FIFO bijection): SOUND. Proof airtight (Step 3's "earlier D is the j-th D with j<=i-1" argument checked); clean-room exhaustive set-equality + uniqueness + decode at n<=5. 2. Lemma 1 + Cor 1.1 (positional criteria D_i/A_i): SOUND. Both directions checked against the literal biconditional containment convention; criterion == literal on all words n<=4 and n=5 (all nonnesting + 8000 random). 3. Facts 3.1-3.2 + Lemma 2 (sign-word bijection, comparison rule, prefix-interval necessity): SOUND. Fact 3.1 exhaustive n<=7; Lemma 2 exhaustive over all (s,p) pairs n<=6; the h=p_j-1 (resp. +1) witness argument and the q_1<q_j<q_m position triple verified. 4. THEOREM A: SOUND. Exhaustive equivalence (literal-validated avoidance <=> prefix-interval + cond 2) over ALL pairs n<=7 (2,162,160 at n=7, 0 mismatches); the open(o_J) formulation computed directly from raw positions (not via Lemma 3), so it independently agrees with the q_1<o_J<q_K form; both proof directions audited including the t=o_A/t'=q_B position elimination. 5. Lemma 3 (FIFO interval): SOUND. Closers-before-o_J form an initial segment q_1..q_c (verified explicitly), open(o_J)={c+1..J-1}, height=J-1-c, late<=>J>=a+1: all Dyck words n<=9. 6. Lemma 4 (obstructions): SOUND. beta=a+1<beta'=a+2 identification correct (gap openers are a prefix of the post-q_1 opener list); 1<=c<=c'<=a-1 verified; tail case: K'=J-1 late via q_{K'}>o_J>q_a, K=J-2 in open(o_{K'}) via o_K<o_{K'}<q_K, eps_K defined since K>=c+1>=a+1>=2 (Observation 4.1). Brute force: every excluded shape n<=9 carries zero valid sign words. 7. Lemma 5 (shape classification S/I/II): SOUND. Both directions verified with every internal claim asserted (forced D-run reconstruction; Tail scan; first-ascent, gap and tail data of constructed words) on all admissible shapes n<=9; parameter map bijective (no duplicates, onto, round-trips); edge cases a=n-1/Tail(1;)=D, i=1, i=a-1, empty tails all hit; S/I/II mutually exclusive and the hypothesis is exactly the negation of Lemma 4's obstruction, so together they exhaust all Dyck words. 8. Lemma 6 + Cor 6.1: SOUND. open(o_{a+1})=empty in (I) and the singleton {J-1} identification verified; local predicate == brute cond-2 validity for every admissible shape n<=8; restriction to F(s) injective with |valid|=2^|F(s)| everywhere; extension map's processing order has well-defined references (j-1 in F, block, a+1, or earlier tail arc). 9. Lemma 7 (language count): SOUND. Predicate == complement(X1 union X2) on every string of the cube n<=10; disjointness and (m,k)-uniqueness hold; counts match 3^n-3*2^(n-1)+1 for n=1..10 including n=1. 10. Theorem B step 1: SOUND. All images in W_n, three cases pairwise disjoint; the case-II witness (r,t)=(i+1,a+1) valid since block nonempty (i<=a-1) and sigma_{a+1} a sign letter; verified n<=8. 11. Theorem B step 2 (parse on W_n): SOUND. The maximal-run/witness two-case argument (r<=a vs r>a) is correct as written; parse total and single-valued on all of W_n for n<=8 (no assertion ever fires); recovered 1<=i<=a-1<=n-2 always; empty A,B-run (i=1) handled; Codex hostile pass concurs. 12. Theorem B steps 3-4: SOUND. Two-sided inverse verified exhaustively n<=8; the (I)-case one-slot shift consistent between Phi and Psi; recovery induction has sound base in both cases (eps_{a+1} from sigma_a in (I), from sigma_{a+1} in (II)). 13. Main theorem (Section 9): SOUND. Middle set = Phi's domain because Lemma 4 + Lemma 5 exactly partition all Dyck words (logical complement verified, plus brute force n<=9); sum of valid eps over shapes = formula n<=8; end-to-end parameter-side reconstruction reproduces the independent DFS avoider files EXACTLY for n<=10 with each word re-verified as an avoider by literal-validated checks.
audits.notes: Adversarial combinatorialist audit; no breaking word found despite targeted attack. METHOD: (a) line-by-line logical audit of every proof; (b) fully clean-room re-implementation of every object (literal containment, FIFO, sign words, open-sets from raw positions, Tail scan, classification, Phi/Psi, W_n predicate) at C:\Users\jacks\source\repos\maths\problems\p3-moonshot\elizalde-luo\work\audit_bij_comb\audit.py and thmA_n7.py, all PASS (logs: thmA_n7.log): Theorem A exhaustive n<=7 (2,162,160 pairs), Lemmas 3-5 all Dyck words n<=9, Lemma 6/Cor 6.1 n<=8, Lemma 7 n<=10, Theorem B n<=8, end-to-end vs independent avoider files n<=10 (the n=9,10 files postdate the paper's n<=8 check and were generated independently of the shape classification); (c) Codex hostile referee (thread 019eba38-7922-7a83-a4a7-ffce3d4a0ebb) on the two subtlest steps (Lemma 4 tail-case + Lemma 4/5 exhaustiveness glue; Theorem B step 2 parse + step 3 induction): verdicts SOUND/SOUND, rest SOUND, no BREAK/GAP. MINOR (non-fatal, for the Lean pass): (1) Lemma 4 tail case uses eps_K, eps_{K'} without restating K>=2; it follows from Observation 4.1 (K in open(o_J), J late) or from K=J-2>=c+1>=a+1 since o_J>q_a implies c>=a>=1 - one explicit sentence recommended. (2) Cor 6.1's parenthetical list of where eps_{j-1} lives is case-dependent ((I) has no block and no a+1 special slot per se since {2..a+1} subset F); harmless but should be split by case when formalized. (3) Lemma 5 case (I) prose 'the scan's first emission for r=1 is D^0 U... preceded by D^a' is compressed; the Dyck/first-ascent/no-gap checks are all verified numerically and easy to formalize. None of these affect correctness. The two formulations of Theorem A's condition (open(o_J) vs q_1<o_J<q_K) agree: my code computes open sets directly from positions, bypassing Lemma 3, and matches.

audits.lemmaVerdicts:
1. Lemma 0 (FIFO bijection): SOUND. Clean-room exhaustive check n<=6: wd(s,p) always nonnesting, round-trips (s,p), count = n!*Cat_n; set equality vs literal 1221/2112 definition n<=5. Proof prose (Steps 1-3, incl. the "earlier position carries p_j, j<i" argument) airtight.
2. Lemma 1 + Cor 1.1 (D_i/A_i criteria): SOUND. Tested on ALL words (not just nonnesting, matching the lemma's hypothesis): exhaustive n<=4, 20,500 random words n=5..7, zero mismatches vs literal biconditional containment. Both proof directions check (i2=snd(v) forced by equal pattern letters).
3. Facts 3.1-3.2 + Lemma 2: SOUND. Fact 3.1 bijection and Fact 3.2 comparison rule exhaustive over all n! perms, n<=7. Lemma 2 (avoider => prefix-interval) exhaustive over all (s,p) n<=6 and on all 85,514 independent-DFS avoiders n<=10.
4. THEOREM A: SOUND. Exhaustive predicate==avoidance over all n!*Cat_n pairs for n<=7 (2,162,160 at n=7), via my own positional fast checks independently validated against the literal definition. The open(o_J) formulation and recurrence-structural.md's (C) "K<J, q_1<o_J<q_K" formulation generate IDENTICAL constraint-pair sets on every Dyck word n<=9 (verified directly; equivalence is immediate since K<J <=> o_K<o_J).
5. Lemma 3 (FIFO interval): SOUND. All Dyck words n<=9, every arc J: open(o_J)=={c+1..J-1} computed from the raw definition; height before J-th U == J-1-c; closers before o_J are exactly the initial segment q_1..q_c; late (o_J>q_1) iff J>=a+1.
6. Lemma 4 (obstructions): SOUND. All Dyck words n<=8: every shape with >=2 gap openers or a tail opener at height >=2 kills all 2^(n-1) sign words (brute force). All identification sub-claims verified on every instance: first two gap arcs are exactly a+1,a+2; a in open(o_beta); {a,beta} subset open(o_beta'); 1<=c<=c'<=a-1; tail case K=J-2,K'=J-1 in open(o_J), K' late (K'>a), K in open(o_{K'}).
7. Lemma 5 (S/I/II classification): SOUND. All Dyck words n<=9: every non-obstructed shape classifies into exactly one case; reconstruction from (a,delta)/(a,i,delta) reproduces s; converse parameter sweep yields valid Dyck words, no collisions, and exactly the admissible set (exhaustiveness with Lemma 4 confirmed: BAD union S/I/II = all shapes). Edge cases a=n-1 (Tail(1;)=D), a=1, i=1, i=a-1, empty tails all exercised. Codex (hostile, thread 019eba3b-49ec-7201-b03c-4e0147c393c5) independently stress-tested classification/reconstruction/converse to n<=14: SOUND, forced-D-run argument certified airtight.
8. Lemma 6 + Cor 6.1: SOUND. All admissible shapes n<=8, all 2^(n-1) eps: direct Theorem-A validity == stated local form (open(o_{a+1})=empty in case I and singleton {J-1} cases fall out of Lemma 3, verified); restriction to F(s) is bijective (count==2^|F|, restrictions distinct). Refined: per-shape 2^|F(s)| matches the independent Rust per-shape counts (refined_stats.json) for every shape n<=8, and the per-shape histogram of the independent DFS avoider lists at n=9,10.
9. Lemma 7 (language count): SOUND. Full 3^n cube n<=10: bullet predicate == complement description; X1, X2 disjoint and cover the complement; |X1|=2^(n-1), |X2|=2^n-1, |W_n|=3^n-3*2^(n-1)+1. (m,k) uniqueness and n=1 case confirmed.
10. Theorem B step 1 (Phi into W_n, disjoint images): SOUND. Every Phi image n<=8 lands in W_n under BOTH the predicate and the complement test; case-II witness (r,t)=(i+1,a+1) always available (block nonempty since i<=a-1, sigma_{a+1} a sign letter); injectivity holds globally.
11. Theorem B step 2 (Psi total on W_n): SOUND. Psi parsed every sigma in W_n for n<=8 with zero assertion failures (asserts encode exactly the draft's claims: first C-run after a leading C ends at a<=n-1 with sigma_{a+1} in {A,B}; 1<=i<=a-1<=n-2); outputs always admissible+valid. Codex certified the witness-pair two-case argument and stress-tested the parse on full cubes n<=12.
12. Theorem B steps 3-4 (two-sided inverse): SOUND. Psi(Phi(s,eps))=(s,eps) for every valid pair n<=8 and for all 85,514 avoiders n<=10 (via independent DFS lists); Phi(Psi(sigma))=sigma for every sigma in W_n, n<=8. The case-(I) one-slot shift and the forced-coordinate induction (incl. j=a+2 referencing arc B, all-C tails) verified letter-perfect.
13. Main theorem (Section 9): SOUND. End-to-end word-level bijection avoiders -> W_n exhaustive n<=7 and on independent DFS data n=8..10 (image exactly W_n, injective, full inverse to the word); no BAD shape carries a valid eps (brute force n<=8); counts = 3^n-3*2^(n-1)+1 for n=1..10, with ground truth re-derived clean-room from the LITERAL containment definition exhaustively for n<=5 (196 at n=5, zero mismatches).
audits.notes: Computationalist audit, fully clean-room: all checkers re-implemented from DEFINITIONS.md and the lemma statements alone (no reuse of project enumerator code), in C:\Users\jacks\source\repos\maths\problems\p3-moonshot\elizalde-luo\work\audit-comp-bij\ (audit_core.py + run_audit.py T1-T8, run_audit2.py T9-T11, run_audit3.py T12-T14, run_literal_n5.py). Every numeric claim in the draft's Section 11 log was independently reproduced; additionally tested on refined data the draft never used: (a) per-Dyck-shape avoider counts vs 2^|F(s)| against data/refined_stats.json (Rust) for all shapes n<=8; (b) full bijection + per-shape histograms on the pruned-DFS avoider files explore/avoiders_n{7..10}.txt (these were generated independently of the shape classification). Section 10's n=3 table (all 16 codes) and the CBCCAC worked example verified verbatim. The draft's open(o_J) formulation of Theorem A and recurrence-structural.md's q_1<o_J<q_K formulation were proven to generate identical constraint sets on all Dyck words n<=9, so the shared-foundation claim holds. Codex (GPT-5.5, hostile referee, thread 019eba3b-49ec-7201-b03c-4e0147c393c5) attacked the two weakest prose steps (Lemma 5 forced reconstruction/converse/exhaustiveness; Theorem B steps 2-3 parse degeneracies and recovery induction): verdict SOUND on both, with its own clean-room stress tests to n<=14 (Lemma 5) and n<=12 (parse/roundtrip). No counterexample, no gap, not even a prose defect found beyond those already fixed in the draft's Section 14 list. I could not break this proof: every quantifier in Lemmas 3-6 ranges over local configurations (first ascent, one gap opener, height<=1 tails) that are fully exercised well below the tested ranges, so the exhaustive verification windows genuinely cover the inductive structure. Recommendation: this draft is ready to be the primary write-up; the only remaining work is Lean formalization, for which the explicit letter-by-letter Phi/Psi and the finite case analyses are well suited.
